docs.title: Fudge
docs.text:
This is a cross-platform open-source alternative to Fujifilm's official camera app.

This app isn't finished yet, so don't set high expectations. Fuji's implementation of PTP/IP has many different quirks and bugs, so it's not easy to create a stable client that works for all cameras.

docs.title: Improvements over XApp / Camera Connect
docs.text:
• Location and notification permissions are not required
  • Much more responsive native UI
  • Performance improvements
  • USB-OTG connectivity support
  • Lua plugins API

Beta builds are published on Google Play. The latest builds are also available on F-Droid.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
• Stable PTP/IP communication with camera over WiFi
  • Tested & working on X-A2, X-H1
  • Tested & working on a few virtual cameras
  • Gallery of images
  • Zoomable image viewer, download/share photos
  • Implement "select multiple / single" mode
  • Implement PTP/USB OTG support
  • Lua scripting
  • AutoSave and tether mode
  • Mass photo import (different folder for each camera?)
  • Camera properties (ISO, white balance, film sim, etc)
  • Implement Bluetooth communication
  • Liveview & Remote capture
  • Desktop app/utility
  • iOS port (see ios/)
  • Translate UI
  • Support landscape mode

docs.title: iOS support?
docs.text: I ported all of Fudge's PTP code to iOS over a weekend, see /ios. It's completely missing a frontend, and that's something I probably won't have the time to make. There is also Fuji-Fi and Cascable. Both are closed-source.

docs.title: Bluetooth?
docs.text:
• If you are interested in having a seperate piece of hardware for connecting to your camera, take a look at furble.
  • I've chosen SimpleBLE for implementing the BLE client code. It's cross-platform, portable, and will be easy to add to Fudge.
  • I plan on implementing a virtual BLE camera for debugging/black-box testing, the same as I did for PTP.

docs.title: Transfer RAW photos?
docs.text:
• Normal 'Wireless Communication' mode doesn't allow RAW photos to be viewed or downloaded. This is hardcoded and cannot be changed.
  • 'PC AutoSave' allows downloading RAWs over an existing WiFi network.
  • If you want the fastest transfer speeds... just use a USB-C sdcard reader

docs.title: About
docs.text:
• The Fudge project was started in June of 2023
  • This project's old name was 'fujiapp' (hence the package name being dev.danielc.fujiapp)
  • 'libfudge' is portable and based on camlib, a PTP client library I wrote.

A lot of Fudge discussion happens in the Fujihack Discord server. We talk about photography, camera mods, and anything related to imaging. Come join if that sounds interesting!

docs.title: Finding the wireless menu
docs.text:
• Look for 'WIRELESS TRANSFER' or 'WIRELESS COMMUNICATION' in 'PLAYBACK MENU'
  • On some older cameras, the 'Fn' button opens up the WiFi menu by default

docs.title: Pairing over WiFi
docs.text:
Older cameras will have these options:

When you press a connection mode, you will get a screen like this:

If you are connecting for the first time, press (OK) CHANGE. Otherwise your camera will reject connections from new clients.

docs.title: PC AutoSave setup
docs.text:
You can setup PC AutoSave from:

  • 'SET-UP' -> 'PC AUTO SAVE SETTING'
  • 'CONNECTION SETTING' -> 'PC AUTO SAVE' -> 'PC AUTO SAVE SETTING'

SIMPLE SETUP: Press the WPS button on your router for 3 seconds and wait until your camera confirms a connection.

MANUAL SETUP: Select your WiFi network and punch in password on the camera.
